desc: Expand references --symbol candidate selection to include incoming SCIP is_reference relationships as well as outgoing ones, without adding transitive expansion or partial-symbol matching.
done_when: A reference query for symbol B returns non-definition occurrences for symbol A when the loaded traversal has a SCIP relationship owned by A with is_reference=true and target symbol B; the same query still includes the exact queried symbol's own non-definition occurrences; duplicate incoming and outgoing reference relationships do not duplicate reference entries; transitive is_reference relationships are not followed; substring, partial-name, fuzzy, regex, or case-insensitive matches are not introduced; command-level golden coverage proves scip-search references --index <fixture> --symbol <B> exits 0 with no stderr and includes the incoming-related occurrence; make test passes.
scope: internal/query/references candidate-symbol selection and tests, shared traversal fixture/reference golden data only as needed to prove incoming is_reference behavior through the production command path. Out of scope: implementations query behavior, discovery query behavior, traversal API shape changes unless strictly required by the existing RelationshipsTargeting view, CLI shared runtime changes, installer/distribution docs.
